evidence: The authors explicitly state they calculated importance "using the permutation method (Altmann et al., 2010)"; they ranked SNPs by importance and identified TPI ("The SNP with the highest importance value occurs on triosephosphate isomerase"); they corroborated with F_ST analysis ("TPI was highly differentiated between MON and NAP, F ST = 0.44") and independent wild-caught validation ("assigned 169 wild-caught adults back to their collection sites with high accuracy 88.57% and 94.95%"). Since permutation importance lacks the impurity bias and all rankings are validated with conditional F_ST and held-out predictive tests, bias cannot affect the conclusions.
